records.ety: [Neut. sing. of L. noster ours, fr. nos we. See Us.]

defs: A medicine, the ingredients of which are kept secret for the purpose of restricting the profits of sale to the inventor or proprietor; a quack medicine.
Any scheme or device proposed by a quack.
quotes: The incentives of agitators, the arts of impostors and the nostrums of quacks.

defs: Any scheme asserted to solve a problem, but with no objective basis for belief in its effectiveness; esp., in politics, a scheme or proposal likely to prove popular with voters.
